data.what: Flutter-based AI movement screening app that uses phone camera pose estimation to detect biomechanical compensation patterns, maps them along three validated fascial chains (SBL/BFL/FFL) to identify upstream root causes, and generates personalized triage reports with cited evidence
data.audience: Athletes, recreational fitness users, and practitioners (PTs, Rolfers, Pilates instructors)
in_scope: Camera pipeline with MediaPipe BlazePose (33 landmarks)
Real-time skeleton overlay with per-joint confidence colors
4 movement assessments (overhead squat, single-leg balance, overhead reach, forward fold)
Movement flow UI with instructions, rep counter, progress indicator
Joint angle calculation from landmark triplets
SBL/BFL/FFL chain mapping via rule-based decision trees
out_of_scope: sEMG hardware integration and haptic cueing
Spiral Line, Lateral Line, SFL chain mapping
data.problem: Movement screening costs $150-2,000 and requires a trained professional. Current AI tools detect what's happening but can't explain why or connect findings to root causes along fascial chains. Zero cross-citations exist between CV and fascial chain research — the fields have never talked to each other.
data.why: Most athletes never get screened. They find out something's wrong when they get hurt. The clinical reasoning that connects symptoms to upstream drivers lives entirely in practitioners' heads and takes 3-4 visits to surface.
data.end_goal: A working Flutter app (mobile + web) that runs a 5-minute, 4-movement screen from a phone camera, detects compensation patterns, maps them along 3 validated fascial chains, and generates a personalized triage report with cited evidence. Validated against 2-3 clinicians on 10 test subjects across 3 layers (measurement accuracy, chain mapping agreement, recommendation usefulness).
data.complexity_reasoning: Large product with 4 subsystems (camera, movement assessment, logic engine, report) spanning ~22 files and ~7 stories, but domain research is already complete in brainlifts/ and research/ directories.
